# anchor

> Context engineering toolkit for AI applications.

anchor is a Python library for building context pipelines that assemble,
rank, and format context windows for large language models. It provides retrieval,
memory management, token budgeting, formatting, query transformation, ingestion,
evaluation, observability, and an agent framework -- all through a composable,
protocol-driven architecture.

- Repo: https://github.com/artcgranja/anchor
- Docs: https://artcgranja.github.io/anchor
- License: MIT
- Python: >=3.11
- Core dependency: pydantic>=2.8

## Installation

pip install astro-anchor

Optional extras:
  pip install astro-anchor[tiktoken]    # tiktoken tokenizer
  pip install astro-anchor[bm25]        # BM25 sparse retrieval
  pip install astro-anchor[anthropic]   # Anthropic formatter + agent
  pip install astro-anchor[agents]      # Agent framework (Anthropic SDK)
  pip install astro-anchor[flashrank]   # FlashRank reranker
  pip install astro-anchor[pdf]         # PDF parsing
  pip install astro-anchor[otlp]        # OpenTelemetry export
  pip install astro-anchor[cli]         # CLI (typer + rich)
  pip install astro-anchor[all]         # Everything

## Core Concepts

### ContextPipeline
The main orchestrator. Assembles context from multiple sources through a series
of steps, respects token budgets, and formats output for a target LLM.

  pipeline = (
      ContextPipeline(max_tokens=8192)
      .add_step(retriever_step("search", my_retriever))
      .with_memory(memory_manager)
      .with_formatter(AnthropicFormatter())
      .add_system_prompt("You are a helpful assistant.")
  )
  result = pipeline.build(QueryBundle(query_str="What is context engineering?"))

Key methods:
  .add_step(step)              -- add a PipelineStep
  .with_memory(provider)       -- attach a MemoryProvider
  .with_formatter(formatter)   -- set output Formatter
  .with_budget(budget)         -- attach a TokenBudget
  .add_system_prompt(text)     -- add a system prompt item
  .add_callback(callback)      -- register a PipelineCallback
  .with_query_enricher(enr)    -- attach a ContextQueryEnricher
  .build(query) -> ContextResult        -- synchronous build
  .abuild(query) -> ContextResult       -- async build

### ContextItem
Immutable Pydantic model. The atomic unit flowing through the pipeline.
Fields: id, content, source (SourceType), score, priority (1-10),
token_count, metadata, created_at.

### ContextWindow
Token-aware, priority-ranked collection of ContextItems.
Fields: items, max_tokens, used_tokens, metadata.

### ContextResult
Output of pipeline.build(). Contains the formatted prompt, the ContextWindow,
and PipelineDiagnostics (step timings, token utilization, overflow stats).

### QueryBundle
Pydantic model carrying a query through the pipeline.
Fields: query_str, embedding (optional), metadata, chat_history.

### TokenBudget / BudgetAllocation
Fine-grained token allocation by SourceType. Each BudgetAllocation has
source, max_tokens, and priority. Unallocated tokens go to a shared pool.

## Protocols (Extension Points)

All extension points are runtime-checkable typing.Protocol classes.
Implement any protocol to plug custom logic into the pipeline.

  Retriever            -- retrieve(query: QueryBundle) -> list[ContextItem]
  AsyncRetriever       -- async variant
  Reranker             -- rerank(items, query) -> list[ContextItem]
  AsyncReranker        -- async variant
  PostProcessor        -- process(items) -> list[ContextItem]
  AsyncPostProcessor   -- async variant
  Tokenizer            -- count_tokens(text) -> int; truncate_to_tokens(text, max) -> str
  Formatter            -- format(window) -> str | list[dict]
  MemoryProvider       -- get_context_items(priority) -> list[ContextItem]
  QueryTransformer     -- transform(query) -> QueryBundle
  AsyncQueryTransformer
  QueryEnricher        -- enrich(query_str, items) -> str
  RecencyScorer        -- score(entry) -> float
  EvictionPolicy       -- select_for_eviction(entries, count) -> list[str]
  CompactionStrategy   -- compact(entries) -> list[MemoryEntry]
  MemoryExtractor      -- extract(turn) -> list[MemoryEntry]
  MemoryConsolidator   -- consolidate(entries) -> list[MemoryEntry]
  MemoryDecay          -- decay(entry) -> float
  QueryRouter          -- route(query) -> str
  QueryClassifier      -- classify(query) -> str
  CacheBackend         -- get/set/delete/clear
  SpanExporter         -- export(spans) -> None
  MetricsCollector     -- record(metric) -> None

## Pipeline Step Factories

Create PipelineStep instances for use with pipeline.add_step():

  retriever_step(name, retriever)              -- run a Retriever
  filter_step(name, predicate_fn)              -- filter items by predicate
  postprocessor_step(name, postprocessor)       -- run a PostProcessor
  reranker_step(name, reranker)                -- run a Reranker
  query_transform_step(name, transformer)       -- transform the query
  classified_retriever_step(name, classifier, retriever_map)
  graph_retrieval_step(name, graph_memory)      -- retrieve from graph memory
  auto_promotion_step(name, promoter)           -- promote items by importance
  create_eviction_promoter(memory, threshold)   -- factory for auto-promotion

Async variants:
  async_retriever_step, async_postprocessor_step, async_reranker_step

## Memory Types

### SlidingWindowMemory
Keeps the last N conversation turns. Simple and token-efficient.

  memory = SlidingWindowMemory(max_turns=20)

### SummaryBufferMemory
Keeps recent turns verbatim; older turns are compacted into summaries.
Requires a CompactionStrategy.

  memory = SummaryBufferMemory(
      max_entries=100,
      compaction_strategy=my_strategy,
  )

### SimpleGraphMemory
Entity-relation-entity triples extracted from conversation. Supports
graph traversal queries.

  graph = SimpleGraphMemory()
  graph.add_triple("Alice", "works_at", "Acme Corp")
  items = graph.query("Alice")

### MemoryManager
Orchestrates one or more memory backends. Implements MemoryProvider.

  manager = MemoryManager(backends=[sliding, summary, graph])

## Retrieval Types

### DenseRetriever
Vector similarity search. Requires an embed function and a list of items.

### SparseRetriever
BM25 keyword retrieval. Requires the `bm25` extra.

### HybridRetriever
Combines dense + sparse retrieval with reciprocal rank fusion (rrf_fuse).

### Routed Retrieval
  RoutedRetriever      -- routes queries to different retrievers
  KeywordRouter        -- route by keyword matching
  MetadataRouter       -- route by query metadata
  CallbackRouter       -- route by custom function

### Rerankers
  ScoreReranker        -- re-score by a custom function
  CrossEncoderReranker -- cross-encoder model reranking
  CohereReranker       -- Cohere rerank API
  FlashRankReranker    -- local FlashRank reranking
  RoundRobinReranker   -- interleave from multiple sources
  RerankerPipeline     -- chain multiple rerankers

## Formatters

### AnthropicFormatter
Formats ContextWindow into Anthropic Messages API format (system + messages).

### OpenAIFormatter
Formats ContextWindow into OpenAI Chat Completions API format.

### GenericTextFormatter
Formats ContextWindow as plain text with section headers.

### BaseFormatter
Abstract base class. Subclass for custom formatting.

## Token Budget Presets

  default_chat_budget(max_tokens)   -- system 10%, memory 10%, conversation 20%, retrieval 25%, reserve 15%
  default_rag_budget(max_tokens)    -- system 10%, memory 5%, conversation 10%, retrieval 40%, reserve 15%
  default_agent_budget(max_tokens)  -- system 15%, memory 10%, conversation 15%, retrieval 20%, tool 15%, reserve 15%

## Query Transformation

  HyDETransformer             -- Hypothetical Document Embeddings
  MultiQueryTransformer        -- generate multiple query variants
  DecompositionTransformer     -- break complex queries into sub-queries
  StepBackTransformer          -- generate a more general "step-back" query
  QueryTransformPipeline       -- chain multiple transformers
  ConversationRewriter         -- rewrite query using chat history
  ContextualQueryTransformer   -- enrich query with context

## Ingestion Pipeline

### DocumentIngester
Parses documents, chunks them, enriches metadata, and stores them.

Parsers: PlainTextParser, MarkdownParser, HTMLParser, PDFParser
Chunkers: FixedSizeChunker, RecursiveCharacterChunker, SemanticChunker,
           SentenceChunker, ParentChildChunker, CodeChunker, TableAwareChunker
Utilities: MetadataEnricher, ParentExpander, generate_doc_id, generate_chunk_id

## Agent Framework

### Agent
High-level agent combining ContextPipeline + Anthropic SDK + tool loop.
Supports streaming chat, automatic tool use, memory, and skills.

  agent = (
      Agent(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")
      .with_system_prompt("You are a helpful assistant.")
      .with_memory(memory)
      .with_tools(memory_tools(memory))
  )
  for chunk in agent.chat("Hello!"):
      print(chunk, end="", flush=True)

Key methods:
  .with_system_prompt(text)     -- set the system prompt
  .with_memory(manager)         -- attach MemoryManager
  .with_tools(tools)            -- register AgentTools
  .with_skills(skills)          -- register Skills
  .chat(message) -> Iterator    -- synchronous streaming chat
  .achat(message) -> AsyncIterator

### @tool decorator
Define tools from functions:

  @tool
  def search_docs(query: str) -> str:
      """Search the documentation."""
      return retriever.retrieve(query)

### Skill / SkillRegistry
Skills are reusable tool bundles that can be activated/deactivated at runtime.

  skill = Skill(name="rag", tools=[search_tool, summarize_tool])
  registry = SkillRegistry()
  registry.register(skill)

Built-in skill factories:
  memory_tools(manager) -> list[AgentTool]   -- memory read/write/search tools
  rag_tools(retriever) -> list[AgentTool]    -- RAG retrieval tools
  memory_skill(manager) -> Skill
  rag_skill(retriever) -> Skill

## Evaluation

  RetrievalMetricsCalculator   -- precision, recall, MRR, NDCG
  LLMRAGEvaluator              -- LLM-judged faithfulness, relevance, coherence
  PipelineEvaluator            -- end-to-end pipeline evaluation
  BatchEvaluator               -- batch evaluation over datasets
  ABTestRunner                 -- A/B test two pipeline configurations

## Observability

  Tracer / TracingCallback     -- distributed tracing for pipeline steps
  Span / SpanKind              -- span model
  CostTracker / CostTrackingCallback -- track LLM API costs

Exporters: ConsoleSpanExporter, FileSpanExporter, InMemorySpanExporter,
           OTLPSpanExporter, OTLPMetricsExporter
Collectors: InMemoryMetricsCollector, LoggingMetricsCollector

## Storage Backends

  InMemoryContextStore    -- in-memory context item store
  InMemoryDocumentStore   -- in-memory document store
  InMemoryVectorStore     -- in-memory vector store
  InMemoryEntryStore      -- in-memory memory entry store
  JsonFileMemoryStore     -- JSON file-backed memory persistence

## Multi-Modal Support

  MultiModalConverter    -- convert multi-modal content to ContextItems
  ModalityType           -- text, image, table, code, etc.
  CompositeEncoder       -- encode multiple modalities
  ImageDescriptionEncoder, TableEncoder, TextEncoder

## Quick Start Example

  from anchor import (
      ContextPipeline, QueryBundle, AnthropicFormatter,
      DenseRetriever, SlidingWindowMemory, MemoryManager,
      retriever_step, default_rag_budget,
  )

  # Set up retrieval
  retriever = DenseRetriever(embed_fn=my_embed, items=my_items)

  # Set up memory
  memory = MemoryManager(backends=[SlidingWindowMemory(max_turns=20)])

  # Build pipeline
  pipeline = (
      ContextPipeline(max_tokens=8192)
      .add_step(retriever_step("search", retriever))
      .with_memory(memory)
      .with_formatter(AnthropicFormatter())
      .with_budget(default_rag_budget(8192))
      .add_system_prompt("You are a helpful assistant.")
  )

  # Execute
  result = pipeline.build(QueryBundle(query_str="How does context engineering work?"))
  print(result.formatted_output)  # Ready for LLM API call
